10_att.c_d: This lesson or case study is woefully short of information, detail and context.  In the most basic sense, I guess there is a business lesson that if you cant/wont focus on improving a mediocre quality product, becoming best in class re logistics, technology to access and customer customisation may be a substitute strategy to win back market share, but where is the info on how this was achieved - what tech improvements looked like, how customer appetite for online ordering evolved(trends etc), what was the messaging to customers looking like (and what were the measurements) to underpin the counterintuitive expectation that a struggling brand with a poor product could possibly succeed to this level?  Also info on competition, how did they manage to win here? Versus not just other pizza makers but other fast food - what was going in in the market to allow this slingshot of sales growth to prosper?  Next to useless without 100 times more detail! - Please select the middle option.
